<!-- DRAFT: Generated by /adev-init. Review and refine. -->

# Architecture: adev-plugin

## Overview

adev-plugin is a Claude Code plugin distributed as an npm package (`adev-cli`). It has four main subsystems: the CLI installer, skills (markdown instructions), hooks (bash lifecycle scripts), and templates (scaffolding files). There are zero external dependencies — everything runs on Node.js built-ins.

## Directory Map

```
adev-plugin/
├── cli/index.mjs              # Single-file CLI — install, scaffold, conflict detection
├── skills/                     # 17 skill directories, each with a SKILL.md
│   ├── using-adev/             # Gateway skill — injected at session start
│   ├── adev-init/              # This wizard
│   ├── adev-brainstorm/        # Idea exploration → Feature Charter
│   ├── adev-specify/           # Write Live Specs within a charter
│   ├── adev-review-specs/      # Parallel architecture review (3 specialist subagents)
│   ├── adev-plan/              # Decompose specs into implementation tasks
│   ├── adev-route/             # Score tasks for auto/assisted/human routing
│   ├── adev-implement/         # Execute tasks with TDD and specialist routing
│   ├── adev-validate/          # Post-implementation quality checks
│   ├── adev-eval/              # Graduated evaluation harness
│   ├── adev-debug/             # Context-aware systematic debugging
│   ├── adev-recover/           # Agent recovery when stuck
│   ├── adev-sample/            # Golden sample curation
│   ├── adev-retro/             # Sprint retrospective
│   ├── adev-hygiene/           # Context staleness and drift audits
│   ├── adev-repomap/           # AST-based symbol index generation
│   └── adev-sync/              # Sync constitution to agent files
├── hooks/                      # 4 bash scripts + hooks.json config
│   ├── hooks.json              # Hook registration (which events trigger which scripts)
│   ├── session-start.sh        # SessionStart — injects using-adev skill
│   ├── constitution-linter.sh  # PreToolUse:Edit — validates constitution constraints
│   ├── merge-guard.sh          # PreToolUse:Bash — enforces merge policy from manifest
│   └── sync-trigger.sh         # PostToolUse:Edit — notifies to run /adev-sync
├── templates/                  # 12 scaffold templates consumed by /adev-init
├── tests/                      # Node.js built-in test runner (node:test)
│   ├── helpers.mjs             # Shared test utilities (temp dirs, fixtures, runHook)
│   ├── cli.test.mjs            # CLI unit tests
│   └── hooks/                  # Per-hook integration tests
├── .claude-plugin/
│   └── plugin.json             # Plugin registration (name, version, metadata)
├── package.json                # npm package config (version must match plugin.json)
└── CLAUDE.md                   # Agent instructions (synced from constitution)
```

## Data Flow

1. **Installation**: User runs `npx adev-cli init` → CLI copies plugin to `~/.claude/plugins/cache/agentic-development/adev/<version>/` and makes hooks executable.

2. **Session lifecycle**: On session start, `session-start.sh` reads `using-adev/SKILL.md` and injects it as context, establishing methodology awareness.

3. **Guard rails**: On every `Edit` tool call, `constitution-linter.sh` validates the constitution file stays under 200 lines and has required sections. On every `Bash` call, `merge-guard.sh` checks the manifest's merge policy and protected branches.

4. **Scaffolding**: `/adev-init` (this skill) creates `.context-index/` in target projects using templates from `templates/`. Templates are copied verbatim via `cpSync()`.

5. **Sync**: After constitution edits, `sync-trigger.sh` fires and reminds to run `/adev-sync`, which propagates the constitution to CLAUDE.md and other agent files listed in the manifest.

## Skill Lifecycle Order

Skills follow a strict pipeline: init → brainstorm → specify → review-specs → plan → route → implement → validate → eval. Supporting skills (debug, recover, sample, retro, hygiene, repomap, sync) can run at any point.

## Key Design Decisions

- **Skills are markdown, not code** — they are portable across AI tools and contain no executable logic (companion code is allowed but not required).
- **Hooks are bash** — they execute in the shell, read JSON from stdin and `CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_*` env vars, and communicate via exit codes and JSON stdout.
- **Templates are static** — changes to templates only affect newly scaffolded projects, not existing ones.
- **Single-file CLI** — all CLI logic lives in `cli/index.mjs` (~400 lines) to keep the codebase simple.
